guards: Report must remain read-only even when module health warnings are found.
The default profile should route to high-signal sections before exposing high-volume module detail.
Operational-compression measures must remain descriptive and advisory, not a score, dashboard, workflow engine, or host-specific tracker policy.
Unknown sections should be treated as usage errors.
Exact field selectors must operate on the selected report payload and must not force a lazy section to build the full report.

steps.description: Execute the operation through the generated TypeScript domain operation table.

ir_plan.summary: Generated TypeScript native runtime binding for a command whose source operation has not yet been decomposed into portable IR.

proof: operational-compression section selector returns compact advisory measures
sectioned exact field selection returns selected-output JSON without full-report assembly

reads.surface: existing report, planning, memory, ownership, and proof-routing summaries
verbose flag for broad diagnostic output
section name and exact field selector for compact drill-down

steps.description: Assemble combined report, startup guidance, and advisory operational-compression measures from existing loaded state.
Emit the default router, verbose detail, one top-level section, or selected exact JSON fields.

summary: Show a compact combined workspace report router by default, with explicit verbose diagnostics, operational-compression measures, section access, and exact field selection for deeper detail.
